confirmed.title: RichTextInput toolbar BUTTON_SPECS labels hardcoded English (aria-label + title)
confirmed.suggested_key: arqel.fields_advanced.richtext_bold / richtext_italic / richtext_underline / richtext_strike / richtext_h1 / richtext_h2 / richtext_h3 / richtext_ul / richtext_ol / richtext_blockquote / richtext_link / richtext_code / richtext_image
confirmed.detail: The BUTTON_SPECS map (lines 162-176) defines label values 'Bold', 'Italic', 'Underline', 'Strikethrough', 'Heading 1', 'Heading 2', 'Heading 3', 'Bullet list', 'Numbered list', 'Blockquote', 'Link', 'Inline code', 'Image' as literal English strings. At line 375 `aria-label={spec.label}` and line 376 `title=...spec.label` these become the visible tooltip AND the accessible name of every formatting button. No translator is applied (the component imports useArqelTranslations and uses it only for the over-limit message and char counter). Round 4 keyed MarkdownInput's toolbar aria-labels, but RichTextInput's parallel toolbar was missed. visible: tooltip title + aria-label.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real, novel, user-facing i18n defect in a first-party (non-vendored) component. In /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx, BUTTON_SPECS (lines 162-176) hardcodes English labels ('Bold','Italic','Underline','Strikethrough','Heading 1/2/3','Bullet list','Numbered list','Blockquote','Link','Inline code','Image'). At line 375 these flow into aria-label={spec.label} and at line 376 into title=...spec.label, so they are both the visible tooltip and the accessible name of every formatting toolbar button. The 'Image upload not configured' title (line 376) is also hardcoded. The component imports useArqelTranslations (line 30) and uses t() for the toolbar role label (359), over-limit message (387) and char counter — but never for the BUTTON_SPECS labels, proving the omission rather than an intentional design.\n\nNot a ledger duplicate (R1-R8): the only RichTextInput entries are Round 7, covering exclusively the max-length error message and the 'N / M' character counter — not the toolbar buttons. The Round 4 entry is filed against packages-js/fields-advanced/src/markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx line 430; although its title parenthetically lists 'RichText', the live RichTextInput BUTTON_SPECS toolbar is still entirely English in source, so the defect reproduces and was missed. Not vendored: it lives under packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: RichTextInput 'Image upload not configured' button title hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: `title={imageDisabled ? 'Image upload not configured' : spec.label}` — when the image upload route is not configured the disabled image button surfaces a hardcoded English tooltip. No t() call. visible: tooltip title.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx:376: `title={imageDisabled ? 'Image upload not configured' : spec.label}`. The string is hardcoded English with no t() wrapper. The translation function IS in scope (`const t = useArqelTranslations()` at line 191, used at lines 359/387), so omitting it here is a genuine i18n gap. The `imageDisabled` state is reachable at runtime (`props.imageUploadRoute === null`, a documented valid value per the prop type at line 54 and the null-handling at lines 313/325/403), so the disabled image button and its English tooltip really render. User-facing: a button `title` is a hover tooltip surfaced to the user.

Not a duplicate: the ledger has three RichTextInput-adjacent entries — Round 4 'every aria-label hardcoded' (covers aria-label attributes across editors; the current aria-label={spec.label} is a separate attribute, not this title), and two Round 7 entries for the max-length error (line 384) and character counter (line 393). None covers the `title` tooltip's conditional 'Image upload not configured' string. 

Not vendored: file is in fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: RichTextInput editor placeholder defaults to hardcoded English 'Start writing...'
confirmed.detail: `Placeholder.configure({ placeholder: props.placeholder ?? 'Start writing...' })` — when the field supplies no placeholder, the Tiptap editor shows the literal English 'Start writing...' to the user. The default should be a translated fallback. visible: editor placeholder text.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx:221: `Placeholder.configure({ placeholder: props.placeholder ?? 'Start writing...' })`. When the field supplies no placeholder, the Tiptap editor renders the literal English 'Start writing...' regardless of locale — user-facing visible text. The component already imports/uses a translator `t()` for other strings (line 359 toolbar aria-label, line 387 max-length error) and a locale-aware numberFormatter, proving i18n is the established pattern here; this fallback was simply never routed through t(). NOVEL vs ledger: the ledger's RichTextInput entries are line 384 (max-length error, R7) and line 393 (char counter, R7); the R4 entry covers fields-advanced editor-control aria-labels (Repeater/Wizard/Builder/Markdown/RichText), not the editor's placeholder text. Line 221 / the 'Start writing...' default is not in the 186-entry ledger. NOT vendored — it lives in packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: CommandPalette 'General' fallback category heading hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: `const key = cmd.category ?? 'General';` in groupByCategory — commands without a category bucket under the literal English 'General', which is rendered verbatim as a visible group heading at line 317 (`{group.category}`). Round 8 keyed the palette footer hints but not this category fallback. visible: group section heading.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:111 `const key = cmd.category ?? 'General';` in groupByCategory. Any command lacking a category buckets under the literal English 'General', and line 317 renders `{group.category}` verbatim inside the visible group heading div (className shows uppercase tracking-wide section header). The translator t() is in scope in this file (used at line 301 aria-label), so the fallback should be keyed but is hardcoded English. User-facing: a visible section heading in the command palette. Not vendored: file is under palette/, not shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the ledger's CommandPalette entries cover sr-only h2 (L274), input placeholder (L285), live-region (L291), listbox aria-label (L297) in Round 1, and footer keyboard hints (L356-358) in Round 8 — none touch the 'General' category fallback at L111/L317. The Round 8 PHP-provider entries (ThemeCommandProvider/NavigationCommandProvider categories) cover server-supplied category strings, not this JS-side default-bucket label for category-less commands. Genuine, novel, user-facing, non-vendored.

confirmed.title: CommandPalette 'Recent' category heading hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: `[{ category: 'Recent', commands: recents }]` — the recents group is labeled with the literal English 'Recent', rendered verbatim as a visible group heading at line 317. No t() call. visible: group section heading.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:154 — `[{ category: 'Recent', commands: recents }]` hardcodes the English literal 'Recent' with no t() call. It is rendered verbatim at line 317 (`{group.category}`) inside a visible group section heading <div> (uppercase, tracking-wide). When the query is empty and recents exist, this English heading shows untranslated to all users, so it is genuinely user-facing and reproduces. Not vendored: file lives in src/palette/, not src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the ledger has 6 CommandPalette.tsx entries (lines 274 sr-only h2, 285 placeholder, 297 listbox aria-label in round 1; 356/357/358 footer keyboard hints in round 8) — none cover the 'Recent' category label at line 154/317. Novel, real, non-vendored.

confirmed.title: AiSelectInput provenance badge 'Suggested by AI' / 'Used fallback' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: `{suggestion === 'ai' ? 'Suggested by AI' : 'Used fallback'}` renders inside a role="status" Badge (lines 261-272) as visible, screen-reader-announced text. Hardcoded English; the component imports a translator (round 4 keyed its error strings at line 183) but this provenance badge was missed. visible: badge text + status live region.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/ai/src/AiSelectInput.tsx:271, which renders `{suggestion === 'ai' ? 'Suggested by AI' : 'Used fallback'}` inside a role="status" Badge (lines 261-272). This is visible text in a status live region (screen-reader announced), so it is a genuine user-facing i18n/a11y defect. The component already has the `t` translator in scope (used at lines 192, 215, 217, 231, 253), so the provenance badge being left as raw English literals is a true miss. The ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) contains two AiSelectInput entries — line 183 (round 4, AI field error strings) and line 165 (round 5, 'Missing classify URL' config error) — neither of which covers the line-271 provenance badge; no entry mentions 'Suggested by AI'/'Used fallback' or provenance. It is a first-party component under packages-js/ai/src/, not a vendored shadcn primitive in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* (the Badge primitive is imported, but the hardcoded text is authored here). Real, novel, non-vendored, not in the R1-R8 ledger.

confirmed.title: FieldSearchController abort message 'Field is not searchable.' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: abort(HttpResponse::HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 'Field is not searchable.') — raw English passed to the abort message, which is rendered in the JSON/error response of the BelongsTo autocomplete endpoint. The sibling ExportDownloadController already routes its abort messages through a $this->message('arqel::messages.export.*', fallback) helper; this controller has no translation routing at all (no __()/trans/arqel:: anywhere in the file). Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldSearchController.php:54: abort(HttpResponse::HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 'Field is not searchable.') passes raw English to the abort message, with no __()/trans/arqel:: routing anywhere in the file (line 58 'Field has search disabled.' shares the defect). The controller is route-registered in packages/fields/routes/arqel-fields.php and powers the BelongsTo async-search endpoint, so the message surfaces in the HTTP/JSON error response (user-facing). The sibling ExportDownloadController routes its abort messages through a translation helper, confirming the framework convention this file violates. Not vendored (framework PHP under packages/fields/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Scanned the R1-R8 ledger: it contains many hardcoded-abort findings (ExportDownloadController, LocaleController, audit controllers, ActionController, tenant RequireTenantFeature, and notably FieldUploadController.php at round 8 in the SAME directory), but no entry for FieldSearchController.php — so this is novel, not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: FieldSearchController abort message 'Field has search disabled.' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: abort(HttpResponse::HTTP_FORBIDDEN, 'Field has search disabled.') — same gap as line 54; reaches the BelongsTo search UI verbatim. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldSearchController.php:58: `abort(HttpResponse::HTTP_FORBIDDEN, 'Field has search disabled.');`. The hardcoded English string is passed directly to Laravel's abort() helper as the HTTP response message, with no __()/trans()/Lang wrapping, matching the sibling untranslated message on line 54. The controller is reachable from the BelongsTo relation search UI, so the message is genuinely user-facing. The file is server-side PHP under packages/fields/src — it is NOT a vendored shadcn primitive (those live in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Scanned the R1-R8 ledger (/home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json): zero matches for FieldSearchController, 'search disabled', 'not searchable', or 'Field is not' — the entire file/concern is absent, so this is novel, not a duplicate. Real, novel, user-facing, non-vendored i18n defect.

confirmed.title: AI controllers return hardcoded English JSON 'message' (Forbidden) across all 5 AI field endpoints
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Forbidden'], 403). The identical hardcoded 'Forbidden' literal recurs at AiTranslateController.php:38, AiExtractController.php:36, AiAnalyzeImageController.php:38, AiGenerateController.php:36. None of these AI controllers import or call __()/trans/arqel:: (grep for translation helpers over packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers returns nothing). The message is surfaced to the AI-field UI (toast/inline error). Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED. Reproduces exactly: all 5 AI field endpoints in packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/ return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Forbidden'], 403) with no __()/trans()/translation helper — verified literals at AiClassifyController.php:32, AiTranslateController.php:38, AiExtractController.php:36, AiAnalyzeImageController.php:38, AiGenerateController.php:36 via grep. These are framework controllers (not app code); the 403 message is surfaced to the AI-field UI error path, so genuinely user-facing. Not vendored — it is a PHP controller, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the i18n ledger (rounds 1-8) has zero entries for any packages/ai/ PHP controller — every packages/ai ledger hit is a React component under packages-js/ai/. The same hardcoded-'Forbidden'-JSON-message pattern WAS fixed in R6, but only for the AUDIT package (RecordActivityController.php:60, GlobalActivityLogController.php:77), leaving the AI package's five instances untouched. This both confirms the pattern is a recognized defect by the project's own standard AND establishes this AI-package occurrence as novel/unledgered. High confidence on reproduction and ledger-absence. Note: the 5 occurrences are one finding (identical literal, same authz pattern), so a fix should sweep all five plus consider the other hardcoded English messages in the same files (e.g., 'AI provider request failed', 'ResourceRegistry not bound', etc.) which the finding scopes narrowly to 'Forbidden'.

confirmed.title: AI controllers leak 'ResourceRegistry not bound: ' + raw exception to client JSON
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'ResourceRegistry not bound: '.$e->getMessage()], 404). Recurs at AiTranslateController:45, AiExtractController:43, AiAnalyzeImageController:57, AiGenerateController has the analogous 'Resource [..] not registered' path. English prefix string concatenated with raw exception message, both reach the client untranslated. Framework-hardcoded (and partially an error-disclosure smell).
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against source and ledger.

REPRODUCES: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/AiClassifyController.php:39 returns `new JsonResponse(['message' => 'ResourceRegistry not bound: '.$e->getMessage()], 404)` — a hardcoded English prefix concatenated with a raw exception message, both untranslated. Line 43 ('ResourceRegistry contract mismatch'), line 50 ('Resource [{$resource}] not registered'), line 60 ('Failed to resolve resource fields: '+raw), and line 77 ('AiSelectField [...] not found...') are all hardcoded English JSON error messages reaching the client. The sibling AI controllers (AiTranslate/AiExtract/AiAnalyzeImage/AiGenerate) follow the same pattern.

USER-FACING: Yes — these are HTTP JSON responses consumed by the AI field React components. The 404 'Resource not registered' path is genuinely reachable; the 'not bound'/'contract mismatch' guards are more defensive but still emit untranslated client strings.

NOVEL / NOT IN LEDGER: The i18n ledger (186 entries, rounds 1-8) covers AI-package strings ONLY in the JS/React layer (packages-js/ai/src/*.tsx). The AI PHP controllers (packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/*) are absent from the ledger. The same systemic family (hardcoded English JSON messages in PHP controllers) was swept for audit/actions/export/auth/core controllers in rounds 2/6/8, but these specific ai-package files/lines were never recorded.

NOT VENDORED: PHP file under packages/ai, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Additional note: the controller already hardens the error-disclosure smell for AiException (lines 88-94: report()+generic message) but NOT for the BindingResolutionException concatenation at line 39 or the field-resolution Throwable at line 60, which still leak raw $e->getMessage() — confirming the leak is a real inconsistency, not intentional.

Confidence med rather than high only because the most-leaky 'not bound' path is a near-unreachable broken-install guard; but at least one reachable path ('Resource not registered', 404) is a clear missing-i18n defect.

confirmed.title: AI controllers return hardcoded 'Resource [{resource}] not registered'
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] not registered"], 404). Same literal at AiClassifyController:50, AiTranslateController:56, AiExtractController:54, AiAnalyzeImageController:68. End-user-visible 404 JSON message, no translation. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Reproduced: AiGenerateController.php:42 returns `new JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] not registered"], 404)` with no __()/trans() wrapping, and the same literal appears in AiClassifyController, AiTranslateController, AiExtractController, AiAnalyzeImageController. This is end-user-visible 404 JSON returned by AI field actions when the resource slug is unknown — a real, framework-hardcoded i18n defect of the exact class the ledger already accepts (round-6 audit RecordActivityController/GlobalActivityLogController JSON messages, round-8 ActionController/RequireTenantFeature/FieldUploadController abort messages). It is a genuine PHP backend controller, NOT a vendored shadcn primitive (those live in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Searched the full R1-R8 ledger: AI entries cover only frontend JS strings (rounds 4/5/6/7 AiTextInput/AiImageInput/AiTranslateInput/AiSelectInput/AiExtractInput) plus a Pulse ai-tokens blade — none touch packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/* nor the "not registered" 404 literal. Novel, user-facing, not vendored, not previously ledgered.

confirmed.title: AI controllers return hardcoded 'Failed to resolve resource fields: ' + raw exception
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Failed to resolve resource fields: '.$e->getMessage()], 500). Same at AiClassifyController:60, AiTranslateController:66, AiExtractController:64, AiAnalyzeImageController:78. 500-level English message reaches client. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/AiGenerateController.php:52 — returns new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Failed to resolve resource fields: '.$e->getMessage()], 500), hardcoded English. Same pattern confirmed at AiClassifyController:60, AiTranslateController:66, AiExtractController:64, AiAnalyzeImageController:78. NOT a duplicate: the R1-R8 ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) contains zero packages/ai/* PHP entries — every AI entry is packages-js/ai/*.tsx (React-side button labels, aria/sr-only text, config errors). The PHP AI controllers were never swept. NOT vendored (not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). User-facing: the message is delivered in the JSON 'message' field consumed by the AI field UI error display. Framework demonstrably has i18n (__('arqel::messages...') is used in core ResourceController), so this is a genuine gap. Caveat (severity, not validity): this is a 500 error path triggered only on server-side resource misconfiguration, and it concatenates a raw untranslatable $e->getMessage(); the broader family of controller envelopes ('Forbidden', 'AI provider request failed', 'Resource [x] not registered') is uniformly hardcoded, suggesting backend error messages were intentionally out of the React-focused i18n sweep. Low priority but a real, novel, non-vendored, user-reaching hardcoded-string defect.

confirmed.title: AI controllers return hardcoded '{FieldType} [{field}] not found on resource [{resource}]'
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => "AiSelectField [{$field}] not found on resource [{$resource}]"], 422). Variants per endpoint: AiTranslateController:83 (AiTranslateField), AiExtractController:81 (AiExtractField), AiAnalyzeImageController:95 (AiImageField), AiGenerateController:69 (AiTextField). 422 client-visible, untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed in source. All five PHP AI HTTP controllers return a hardcoded English 422 JSON message with no i18n wrapper:
- AiClassifyController.php:77 -> "AiSelectField [{$field}] not found on resource [{$resource}]"
- AiTranslateController.php:83 -> "AiTranslateField [...] not found on resource [...]"
- AiExtractController.php:81 -> "AiExtractField [...] not found on resource [...]"
- AiAnalyzeImageController.php:95 -> "AiImageField [...] not found on resource [...]"
- AiGenerateController.php:69 -> "AiTextField [...] not found on resource [...]"
Each is returned directly in a JsonResponse with HTTP 422, so it is client/user-facing (browser/API consumer), and uses no __()/trans()/lang key — framework-hardcoded English.

Not a duplicate: the i18n-fixed-ledger.json (186 entries, rounds 1-8) contains ZERO entries for packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/*. Grep for that path against the ledger returns 0 matches. The AI-related ledger items all target the React/JS side (packages-js/ai/AiTextInput.tsx, AiExtractInput.tsx, AiSelectInput.tsx, AiImageInput.tsx, AiTranslateInput.tsx) or other packages' controllers (core, export, auth, audit, actions, fields) — none touch these PHP AI server controllers or this 'not found on resource' message.

Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it is PHP server-side code, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*).

Minor severity caveat: this is an error/misconfiguration path (field-name mismatch) rather than a happy-path user message, but it is still genuinely user-visible and untranslated.

confirmed.title: AI controllers return hardcoded 'AI provider request failed'
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'AI provider request failed'], 422) — the user-facing fallback when the provider call throws. Recurs at AiTranslateController:115, AiExtractController:98, AiAnalyzeImageController:109, AiGenerateController:86. This is the most commonly surfaced AI error toast and is hardcoded English. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED real, novel, not vendored.

Reproduces: grep confirms the literal 'AI provider request failed' hardcoded in 5 framework PHP controllers — AiClassifyController:94, AiGenerateController:86, AiExtractController:98, AiTranslateController:115, AiAnalyzeImageController:109 — each returned as the `message` of a 422 JsonResponse on the AiException provider-throw path.

User-facing: this 422 `message` is the AI error toast shown to the admin user when a provider call fails (the most common AI failure path). The ledger itself documents the JS consumer side (AiSelectInput.tsx 'user-facing error strings hardcoded'), confirming these messages surface in the UI.

Not vendored: path is packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/, framework PHP — not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Not a duplicate (R1-R8): the ledger has 186 entries; zero match 'packages/ai' (PHP path) and zero contain 'provider request failed'. Every prior AI entry targets the JS side (packages-js/ai/src/*.tsx in rounds 4-7) or Pulse blade / command-palette PHP (round 8). The packages/ai PHP HTTP controllers were never swept.

Genuinely untranslatable as written: grep finds zero __()/trans()/Lang usage anywhere in packages/ai/src, whereas packages/core/src uses __()/trans() for user-facing strings (LocaleController, ResourceController, Resource) — so this hardcoded string violates the established framework i18n convention. Fix is to wrap in a translation key (e.g. __('arqel-ai::messages.provider_request_failed')).

Note: the fixed/generic wording is an intentional security hardening (avoid reflecting raw provider response bodies, per the inline comments and PR #211). That intent is correct and orthogonal — it does not require the string to be hardcoded English. The i18n defect stands.

confirmed.title: AiAnalyzeImageController validation message 'Either imageUrl or imageBase64 must be provided' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Either imageUrl or imageBase64 must be provided'], ...). A user-facing input-validation error returned verbatim in English, not routed through validator/__()/trans. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/AiAnalyzeImageController.php lines 46-51: when both imageUrl and imageBase64 are empty, the controller returns new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Either imageUrl or imageBase64 must be provided'], 422). The English string is hardcoded inline, not routed through __()/trans()/a validator with translatable messages. This is a reachable code path: the single-action endpoint is hit by the AiImageInput frontend, and the 422 message body can surface to the end user, so it is genuinely user-facing.

Novel: I scanned the full R1-R8 ledger (/home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json). There is NO entry for packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/AiAnalyzeImageController.php. The ledger's nearest AI entries are all the JS frontend (R5 'Missing translate URL'/'Missing classify URL' in AiTranslateInput/AiSelectInput; R7 AiImageInput.tsx:230 'Missing analyze URL') — a different file (packages-js) and a different message. grep for 'Either imageUrl'/'must be provided'/'AiAnalyzeImageController' across reports/ returned nothing.

Not vendored: file is packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/..., not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Same defect class as multiple previously-accepted ledger findings (R5 GlobalActivityLogController hardcoded JSON validation messages, R6 RecordActivityController invalid-subject message, R8 ActionController 'Missing selection.'), confirming the project treats hardcoded English user-facing validation JSON in PHP controllers as a real i18n defect.

confirmed.title: AI controllers also leak 'ResourceRegistry contract mismatch' to client
confirmed.detail: return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'ResourceRegistry contract mismatch'], 404). Same at AiTranslateController:49, AiExtractController:47, AiAnalyzeImageController:61. Diagnostic English string returned to the client unticketed/untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at all four cited locations: AiClassifyController.php:43, AiTranslateController.php:49, AiExtractController.php:47, AiAnalyzeImageController.php:61 each return `new JsonResponse(['message' => 'ResourceRegistry contract mismatch'], 404)` — a hardcoded English diagnostic string sent to the client, never passed through __() or a translation key. NOT vendored (framework PHP under packages/ai, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). NOT a duplicate: the 186-entry ledger covers core/audit/actions/export/tenant/fields/nav/form controllers but has ZERO packages/ai entries and no 'contract mismatch'/registry-message title. The defect class (hardcoded English in a 4xx JSON `['message' => ...]` controller response) is exactly what the ledger already treats as a valid i18n bug — e.g. RecordActivityController:53/:60 (round 6), FieldUploadController:66 (round 8), ExportDownloadController:41 (round 2) — so it meets the project's own established bar. Confidence is med rather than high because this specific branch only fires on a ResourceRegistry contract mismatch (a misconfiguration/dev condition), so it is less runtime-reachable than the sibling 'Forbidden'/'Resource not registered' messages on adjacent lines; still, it is a genuine, novel, untranslated user-facing string.

confirmed.title: Versioning VersionRestoreController returns hardcoded 'Model does not use the Versionable trait.'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Model does not use the Versionable trait.'], ...). User-visible JSON error on restore attempt; no __()/trans in the controller. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionRestoreController.php:53-55: `new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Model does not use the Versionable trait.'], 422)` — hardcoded English, no __()/trans() anywhere in the controller. This 422 body is returned to the HTTP client on a restore attempt, so it is user-visible. Not in the R1-R8 ledger: no ledger entry references VersionRestoreController.php (versioning entries cover VersionTimeline.tsx, VersionDiff.tsx, and the Version model only). Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it is a framework PHP controller, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. The defect class (hardcoded user-facing JSON message in a framework controller) matches multiple already-accepted ledger entries (GlobalActivityLogController R5/R6, RecordActivityController R6, ActionController/RequireTenantFeature R8), so by the project's own precedent it qualifies. Confidence is med rather than high only because this is a misconfiguration-guard path (fires when a model lacks the Versionable trait) rather than a normal-flow message.

confirmed.title: Versioning VersionRestoreController returns hardcoded 'Restore failed.'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Restore failed.'], ...) — the user-facing failure toast for a version restore, hardcoded English. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionRestoreController.php:104: the catch-all Throwable handler returns `new JsonResponse(['restored' => false, 'message' => 'Restore failed.'], 500)`. The string 'Restore failed.' is hardcoded English with no Laravel translation helper (__()/trans()), and it sits in the response body alongside 'restored' => false as a client-facing failure message (distinct from the separate Log::error diagnostic on line 95). This is framework PHP code, not a vendored shadcn primitive (packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*).

Not a duplicate: the i18n ledger has zero entries for VersionRestoreController.php or any PHP backend versioning file. All versioning ledger entries are frontend TSX in packages-js/versioning/ (VersionTimeline.tsx lines 36/124/179, VersionDiff.tsx lines 124/179/204/206 — rounds 2/4/6). None cover this server-side message.

Caveat (lowers confidence to med): it is user-facing only insofar as the consuming app surfaces the 500 response body as a toast/error; the bundled VersionTimeline.tsx onRestore handler does not itself read the .message field, so reproduction as a visible toast depends on app-level Inertia/axios error handling. Regardless, it is unambiguously a hardcoded English string returned to the client in framework code that should be wrapped in __().

confirmed.title: Versioning VersionHistoryController returns hardcoded 'ResourceRegistry not bound' / 'Forbidden'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'ResourceRegistry not bound'], 404) at lines 49 and 53, plus 'Forbidden' at line 80. Version-history panel error messages returned untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against the actual file and the R1-R8 ledger.

REPRODUCES: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionHistoryController.php returns hardcoded English error strings in user-facing JSON responses: line 49 and 53 `'ResourceRegistry not bound'` (404), line 60 `"Resource [{$resource}] not registered"`, line 64 `"Resource [{$resource}] is invalid"`, line 75 `'Model [...] does not use the Versionable trait'`, line 80 `'Forbidden'` (403). The controller contains ZERO `__()` translation calls (grep count = 0), and the versioning package has no resources/lang directory, so none of these can be localized.

USER-FACING: these JSON `message` payloads are surfaced by the version-history panel; the 403 'Forbidden' is the access-denied message the end user sees.

NOVEL / NOT IN LEDGER: scanned the full i18n-fixed-ledger.json (R1-R8). It contains many PHP-controller entries (audit, actions, export, core, etc.) but NONE for packages/versioning. Critically, the precedent is established: GlobalActivityLogController.php (audit) had its identical patterns fixed in R5 (hardcoded query-validation JSON messages -> __('arqel-audit::messages.*')) and R6 (403 'Forbidden' -> __('arqel-audit::messages.forbidden')). The versioning controller is the same defect class in an un-swept package.

NOT VENDORED: this is a framework-authored PHP controller, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: Marketplace controllers return hardcoded 'Forbidden' JSON message (repeated, ~8 controllers)
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Forbidden'], 403). Identical hardcoded 'Forbidden' at AdminRefundController:25, SecurityScanListController:22, PluginAdminListController:23, PluginReviewModerationController:27 & 73, PluginAdminReviewController:26. No marketplace controller uses __()/trans (grep over packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers returns no translation helper). All surfaced to the marketplace admin UI. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Reproduced exactly. grep over packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers confirms hardcoded ['message' => 'Forbidden'], 403 at PluginFeatureController.php:24 plus SecurityScanListController:22, PluginReviewModerationController:27 & :73, AdminRefundController:25, PluginAdminReviewController:26, PluginAdminListController:23 (7 controllers, 8 occurrences). No __()/trans()/Lang:: anywhere in the marketplace controllers directory (returns NONE). These are 403 JSON messages surfaced to the marketplace admin UI — genuinely user-facing and framework-hardcoded. Not vendored: this is PHP, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Ledger check: the R1-R8 ledger contains the identical hardcoded-'Forbidden' pattern but in a different package (audit: GlobalActivityLogController, RecordActivityController). The only marketplace entries in the ledger are SubmitPluginRequest.php and apps/marketplace app.blade.php — neither is any of the cited controllers. So while the family/pattern was previously addressed in audit, these specific marketplace files were never swept and are novel. Confirmed.

confirmed.title: Marketplace controllers return hardcoded 'Unauthenticated' JSON message (repeated)
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Unauthenticated'], 401). Same literal at PublisherPayoutsController:23, PluginPurchaseController:30/50/107/114, PluginReviewController:28, PluginReviewVoteController:30/107. Surfaced to user; untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real. PluginDownloadController.php:25 returns `new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Unauthenticated'], 401)` — a hardcoded English literal never passed through __()/trans(). The same literal is verified at PublisherPayoutsController:23, PluginPurchaseController:30/50/107/114, PluginReviewController:28, and PluginReviewVoteController:30/107 (grep-confirmed exact matches). These are user-facing JSON API error messages surfaced to authenticated/unauthenticated API clients. It is PHP framework code in packages/marketplace, NOT a vendored shadcn primitive (those live in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Not a duplicate: the R1-R8 ledger covers other controllers' hardcoded JSON messages (audit GlobalActivityLogController/RecordActivityController, core LocaleController, actions ActionController, tenant RequireTenantFeature, fields FieldUploadController, export ExportDownloadController) and a marketplace validation request (SubmitPluginRequest:43), but no entry references PluginDownloadController, PublisherPayoutsController, PluginPurchaseController, PluginReviewController, or PluginReviewVoteController, and no entry references the 'Unauthenticated' literal. Novel, user-facing, reproducible, not vendored.

confirmed.title: Marketplace controllers return hardcoded 'Validation failed' JSON message (repeated)
confirmed.detail: ['message' => 'Validation failed'] returned in JSON. Same at PluginFeatureController:34, AdminRefundController:46 & 53, PluginPurchaseController:120, PluginReviewController:39, PluginReviewModerationController:84, PluginAdminReviewController:37, PluginReviewVoteController:40. A user-facing validation error wrapper that bypasses Laravel's translated validation messages. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified in /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/PluginPurchaseController.php:41 and confirmed via grep across the marketplace controllers: ['message' => 'Validation failed'] hardcoded at PluginPurchaseController:41 & :120, PluginReviewController:39, PluginAdminReviewController:37, PluginReviewModerationController:84, AdminRefundController:46 & :53, PluginReviewVoteController:40, PluginFeatureController:34. These controllers manually construct the 422 JSON envelope instead of throwing Laravel's ValidationException, so the top-level 'message' bypasses Laravel's translated validation messaging (which honors app()->getLocale()/lang/validation.php). The string is returned in the JSON response body to the client, so it is genuinely user-facing. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (these are PHP framework package controllers, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Ledger scan (rounds 1-8) shows only two unrelated marketplace entries: apps/marketplace/.../app.blade.php lang attr (R6) and SubmitPluginRequest messages()/attributes() gap (R7); neither covers this hardcoded 'Validation failed' JSON wrapper. Novel, reproduces, user-facing, not vendored, not in ledger.

confirmed.title: Marketplace PluginDownloadController hardcoded 'License required'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'License required'], 403). User-facing paywall message, untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/PluginDownloadController.php:45: `return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'License required'], 403);`. This is a user-facing JSON paywall message returned to the client on a premium-plugin download attempt without a completed purchase — genuinely user-facing and framework-hardcoded (not behind any trans/__ call). The established codebase pattern, applied across R1-R8 fixes (e.g. packages/export/src/Http/Controllers/ExportDownloadController.php uses `trans('arqel::messages.export.not_found', ...)`), is to localize such messages, so this is a real instance of the same missing-i18n family. Novel: PluginDownloadController.php does NOT appear in the ledger (/home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json); the only marketplace entries are packages/marketplace/src/Http/Requests/SubmitPluginRequest.php and apps/marketplace/resources/views/app.blade.php. Not vendored: it is a backend PHP controller, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. (Note: sibling lines 25 'Unauthenticated' and 31 'Plugin not found' share the same defect but the finding scopes only line 45.)

confirmed.title: Marketplace controllers hardcoded 'Purchase not found' / 'Review not found'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Purchase not found'], 404). 'Review not found' at PluginReviewModerationController:92, PluginReviewVoteController:48 & 113. User-facing 404 messages, untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed all three cited locations reproduce exactly. AdminRefundController.php:41 returns new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Purchase not found'], 404). PluginReviewModerationController.php:92 and PluginReviewVoteController.php:48 both return 'Review not found' (vote controller also has it at ~113). These are user-facing 404 JSON error messages, hardcoded English with no __() translation call — same defect class as numerous already-accepted PHP-controller findings in the ledger (audit GlobalActivityLogController/RecordActivityController r5/r6, export ExportDownloadController r2, LocaleController r2, tenant RequireTenantFeature r8). Not a duplicate: the only marketplace entry in rounds 1-8 is SubmitPluginRequest.php:43 (r7), a FormRequest messages()/attributes() gap in a different file with a different defect mechanism; these three controller files appear nowhere in the ledger. Not vendored: files are framework PHP under packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: Marketplace AdminRefundController hardcoded 'Refund failed at gateway'
confirmed.detail: new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Refund failed at gateway'], ...). User-facing payment-failure message, untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/AdminRefundController.php line 62: `return new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Refund failed at gateway'], 422);` — confirmed present verbatim. It is a user-facing payment-failure message returned in the JSON response after a failed refund, with no i18n wrapping (no __(), trans(), or Lang:: used anywhere in the file). Novel: the ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) contains only two marketplace entries (resources/views/app.blade.php and src/Http/Requests/SubmitPluginRequest.php) and zero entries containing "refund" or referencing AdminRefundController.php. Not vendored: it is framework PHP source under packages/marketplace/src, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Caveat noted for context: every message in this controller is hardcoded English (Forbidden, Purchase not found, Validation failed, etc.), so this is part of a broader untranslated-API-response pattern rather than an isolated slip — but the specific flagged string genuinely reproduces, is user-facing, is novel, and is not vendored.

confirmed.title: Marketplace PluginPurchaseController hardcoded 'Payment verification failed'
confirmed.detail: ['message' => 'Payment verification failed'] returned in checkout-verify JSON. User-facing payment error, untranslated. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/PluginPurchaseController.php:154-157: the checkout-verify action returns new JsonResponse(['message' => 'Payment verification failed', ...], 422) with a hardcoded English string, never passed through __() or trans(). It is genuinely user-facing — returned to the buyer in the payment verification JSON response when gateway verification fails — and untranslated. It is a framework PHP controller, not a vendored shadcn primitive (packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Ledger scan (i18n-fixed-ledger.json, rounds 1-8): the only marketplace entry is round 7 SubmitPluginRequest.php:43 (a different file, about validation :attribute names). No entry exists for PluginPurchaseController.php nor for the 'Payment verification failed' message. Novel, reproduces, not vendored, not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: Workflow StateFilter serializes hardcoded label 'State' bypassing base Filter localization
confirmed.detail: toArray() emits 'label' => 'State' as a raw literal. The base packages/table/src/Filter.php::getLabel() routes labels through localizeLabel()/trans() (the documented localization path used by the already-fixed TernaryFilter/TrashedFilter), but StateFilter does not extend that base and hard-emits its own untranslated label. Plumbing gap: the localization path exists but this filter does not use it, and StateFilter exposes no label() setter to override. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages/workflow/src/Filters/StateFilter.php:76: toArray() unconditionally returns 'label' => 'State', a raw English literal. The class is `final readonly`, does not extend the base Arqel\Table\Filter (it is deliberately standalone/agnostic of arqel-dev/table per its docblock), exposes no label() setter, and contains zero calls to __()/trans()/localizeLabel(). The label is serialized for Inertia and consumed as a filter label by arqel-dev/table, so it is user-facing in the admin UI and renders untranslated regardless of locale — genuinely reproduces.

Novel vs R1-R8 ledger: no entry references packages/workflow/src/Filters/StateFilter.php. The closest entries are (a) round 8 packages/table/src/Filter.php:191 (the BASE table Filter — a different class/file that StateFilter does not extend) and (b) round 6 packages-js/workflow/src/StateTransition.tsx (a React component with different strings 'No state assigned'/'No transitions available'). Neither covers this PHP workflow filter's hardcoded 'State' label.

Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it lives in packages/workflow/src/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. All gates (real, novel, user-facing, non-vendored) pass.

confirmed.title: StatCard numeric KPI value rendered raw without Intl.NumberFormat grouping
confirmed.suggested_key: n/a (use Intl.NumberFormat(locale) on numeric value via useArqelLocale)
confirmed.detail: The big-number widget renders `{value ?? '—'}` directly. `StatWidget::resolveValue()` (packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php:188) returns a raw int|float, e.g. `User::count()` => 1234567. With `tabular-nums` styling but no `Intl.NumberFormat`, the value displays as '1234567' in every locale — no thousands grouping, no locale decimal mark. This is a DISTINCT surface from the table NumberCell fixed in round 2 (cells.tsx): the dashboard StatCard widget never passed value through a locale-aware formatter. Repro: declare StatWidget::make('users')->value(fn()=>1234567), render dashboard under pt-BR locale; expected '1.234.567', actual '1234567'. Float values render with '.' decimal regardless of locale.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified reproduction. PHP StatWidget::resolveValue() (packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php:190-201) returns a raw int|float|string|null with no formatting (e.g. fn()=>1234567 yields integer 1234567). The frontend StatCard renders this verbatim at packages-js/ui/src/widgets/StatCard.tsx:115 — `
{value ?? '—'}
` — with no Intl.NumberFormat call anywhere in the file. Under pt-BR the expected '1.234.567' renders as '1234567'; float values always print with '.' decimal regardless of locale. This is a genuine user-facing i18n defect on a prominent dashboard "big number" widget. NOT a duplicate: the only StatCard ledger entry (round 1, line 90) covers the sparkline aria-label, unrelated to the numeric value. All number-grouping entries in the ledger are distinct surfaces — round 2 line 169 (NumberCell in cells.tsx, table column), round 7 (RichText char counter), round 8 (Pulse blade number_format). The StatCard KPI value at line 115 is genuinely uncovered, and the finding itself correctly distinguishes it from the round-2 cells.tsx fix. NOT vendored: packages-js/ui/src/widgets/StatCard.tsx is a first-party Arqel widget, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Minor caveat: StatCardWidget.value is typed string|number|null, so a developer can pass a pre-formatted string to work around it, but the framework offers no locale-aware path for the idiomatic numeric case shown in the repro, so the defect stands.

confirmed.title: Dashboard TableCard formatCell uses String(value) for numbers — no locale grouping
confirmed.detail: formatCell() does `return String(value)` for `typeof value === 'number'`. Numeric columns in a dashboard TableCard widget (the Recharts-cluster table card, distinct from the main DataTable cells fixed across rounds 1-2) therefore render with no thousands separators and en-US '.' decimals regardless of active locale. Repro: a TableCard row with a numeric cell like 1500000 renders '1500000' under pt-BR instead of '1.500.000'. No `Intl.NumberFormat` and no access to the active locale in this component.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/widgets/TableCard.tsx lines 101-105: formatCell() returns String(value) for numbers (and strings/booleans) with no Intl.NumberFormat and no access to the active locale. Numeric cells extracted verbatim from record[column.name] (per TableWidget::data() contract) therefore render en-US style (no thousands grouping, '.' decimal) regardless of active locale — e.g. 1500000 renders as '1500000' under pt-BR. This is user-facing (dashboard TableCard widget) and reproduces as described.

NOT a duplicate: the ledger's only TableCard.tsx entry is line 89 (round 1, 'See all' link text) — a different concern. The round-2 numeric-format entries are for packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx (NumberCell line 169, DateCell, formatRelative) — a distinct file/component (the main DataTable cell renderer), which the finding explicitly distinguishes itself from. No ledger entry covers formatCell in this widget file.

NOT vendored: located in packages-js/ui/src/widgets/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Caveat (severity): TableCardColumn carries only name+label — no type/format metadata — so the component cannot distinguish a numeric metric (which should be grouped) from a numeric primary key/ID (which should NOT be grouped as '1.500.000'). The component is documented as intentionally schema-light extracting cells verbatim. So while the literal claim reproduces, a 'fix' is non-trivial and the defect is comparatively mild. Still meets the bar: real, user-facing, novel, not vendored.

confirmed.title: Demo dashboard chart labels use ->format('M j') — English month abbreviation hardcoded
confirmed.detail: postsPerDay() builds chart x-axis labels via `$day->format('M j')`, which emits English textual month tokens (Jan, Feb, Mar...) regardless of app()->getLocale(). Under pt-BR the labels still read 'Jun 23' instead of 'Jun 23' localized (e.g. abbreviation differs in many locales) — the textual 'M' token is never translated. Should use Carbon's translatedFormat() (->translatedFormat('M j')) or isoFormat so the month abbreviation honors the locale. This is the example/showcase app but is the reference pattern users copy.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at apps/demo/app/Arqel/Dashboards/MainDashboard.php:76. postsPerDay() builds chart x-axis labels via $day->format('M j'). PHP's native DateTime::format always emits English textual month tokens (Jan, Feb, Jun...) regardless of app()->getLocale(); only Carbon's translatedFormat()/isoFormat() honor the locale. The demo app is authored in pt-BR (e.g. ->description('Resumo da actividade do blog.')), so the chart axis rendering English month abbreviations is a genuine, user-visible localization inconsistency. Not vendored: the file is in apps/demo, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: grep of the R1-R8 ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) for MainDashboard / translatedFormat / postsPerDay / format('M returned no match; the ledger entries are all in packages-js/ui/src/* (DataTable, TableFilters, nativeFields, etc.), none touching this demo dashboard. Caveat lowering severity: this is the example/showcase app rather than a shipped arqel-dev/* package, but it is a reference pattern users copy, and it does reproduce as described. translatedFormat('M j') is the correct fix.

confirmed.title: DevTools TimeTravel snapshot timestamp uses toLocaleTimeString() with no locale arg
confirmed.detail: `{new Date(snap.timestamp).toLocaleTimeString()}` formats time-travel snapshot timestamps with no explicit locale argument, deferring to the browser/OS locale instead of the panel locale. Same low-severity developer-tooling caveat as InertiaInspector.tsx:145; both are the only remaining no-locale toLocale* calls in the JS tree and are not in the fixed ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/TimeTravel.tsx:82: `{new Date(snap.timestamp).toLocaleTimeString()}` — confirmed verbatim. The call passes no locale argument, so it defers to the browser/OS locale instead of the panel locale, producing locale-inconsistent timestamp formatting in the time-travel snapshot list. NOT a duplicate: the i18n-fixed-ledger (rounds 1-8) contains only packages-js/ui/ entries (DataTable, TableFilters, TablePagination, nativeFields, etc.); grep found zero references to TimeTravel, InertiaInspector, devtools-extension, or toLocaleTimeString. NOT vendored: the file lives in devtools-extension/src/panel/, a first-party panel, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/. The codebase has a useArqelLocale hook (packages-js/react/src/utils/useArqelLocale.ts) establishing a panel-locale convention this call bypasses, and these two devtools panels (TimeTravel.tsx:82 + InertiaInspector.tsx:145) are the only remaining no-locale toLocale* calls in the non-vendored JS tree. Real, novel, reproducible locale-consistency defect. Severity is low — it is a browser developer-tooling extension panel (debugging aid for framework developers), and self-describes as a low-severity caveat — but it is genuinely user-facing rendered UI and qualifies.
